message:
fix(fp-frame): preserve column_order on serde round-trip (fd90.196 / br-1d1gm)

Found while writing an integration test for the README's
"Serialization and Interoperability" section (lines 1560-1580):
DataFrame.column_order had `#[serde(skip)]`, which silently dropped
the column-insertion order on JSON round-trip. The README directly
claims types "Round-trip perfectly" — that claim was false.

Repro:
    let df = read_csv_str("a,b\n1,4")?;       // column_order = [a, b]
    let json = serde_json::to_string(&df)?;
    let back: DataFrame = serde_json::from_str(&json)?;
    assert_eq!(df.column_names(), back.column_names());  // BANG: [a,b] != []

Fix:
- Remove `#[serde(skip)]` on column_order so it serializes normally
- Add `#[serde(default)]` so any pre-fd90.196 blob without
  column_order still deserializes (with empty Vec, matching prior
  behavior).

The columns BTreeMap still serializes alphabetically, but the
explicit column_order field now lets users recover the
insertion order.

Also adds:
- serde_json dev-dep on the frankenpandas crate
- readme_serialization_compiles_and_runs covering Scalar (5 variants
  — verifies the documented {"kind":..., "value":...} tagged form),
  IndexLabel, and DataFrame round-trips

Verified: 1434 fp-frame lib tests + 34 integration + 7 frankenpandas
lib + 2 doctests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

+{"id":"frankenpandas-1d1gm","title":"fd90.196: Integration test for README Serialization section (serde_json round-trip)","description":"fd90.196: Add integration test for README "Serialization and Interoperability" section (lines 1560-1580).

The README claims all core types round-trip perfectly through serde_json and that the Scalar enum uses tagged representation. Today no integration test exercises this — the claim could silently break if a derive is dropped or a tag attribute is altered.

Scope:
- Add serde_json as a dev-dependency to crates/frankenpandas/Cargo.toml
- New test: readme_serialization_compiles_and_runs that round-trips:
  - Scalar (each major variant: Bool, Int64, Float64, Utf8, Null) — verify the documented {"kind":..., "value":...} tagged form
  - IndexLabel
  - DataFrame (full round-trip — exercises Index + Column + ValidityMask)
- Verify the round-trip is value-preserving (semantic_eq or PartialEq on outputs)

bincode is mentioned but the README hedges with "or messagepack, etc." — leaving bincode coverage out of scope for this bead to keep dev-dep additions minimal.

Files: crates/frankenpandas/Cargo.toml + crates/frankenpandas/tests/readme_quick_example.rs.
Time estimate: 25-35min.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"test","created_at":"2026-04-26T20:08:14.788817068Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T20:11:17.344972892Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T20:11:17.342961189Z","close_reason":"Added readme_serialization_compiles_and_runs covering serde_json round-trip for Scalar (5 variants — Bool/Int64/Float64/Utf8/Null with assertion that JSON contains 'kind' tag), IndexLabel (Int64 + Utf8), and DataFrame (full round-trip). FOUND AND FIXED A REAL DEFECT: DataFrame.column_order had #[serde(skip)] which silently dropped column ordering on round-trip — directly contradicting the README's 'Round-trips perfectly' claim. Removed the skip and added #[serde(default)] for backwards-compat. Added serde_json as dev-dep. All 1434 fp-frame lib tests + 34 integration + 7 frankenpandas lib + 2 doctests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-1d9y","title":"[HIGH] infra: rust-toolchain.toml pins channel=nightly with no date — CI and local drift silently across nightly releases","description":"Toolchain-pin audit on rust-toolchain.toml + build reproducibility.

**Observation.** rust-toolchain.toml has `channel = "nightly"` with no date pin. No `components` field. No `targets` field.

**Consequence.**
- A developer who installs the repo today gets nightly-2026-04-22.
- A week later, nightly advances to nightly-2026-04-29. Their `cargo build` silently upgrades.
- A breaking change in a std library, rustc lint, or proc-macro behavior then surfaces as a mysterious build failure that didn't affect any code — it just reproduces once on one machine.
- CI runs `dtolnay/rust-toolchain@nightly` (ci.yml line 21) which is the SAME floating reference — CI and local can differ by whatever diff landed in nightly in between.
- rustc version doesn't appear in `cargo --version` output committed artifacts; reproducing a failing CI build locally requires manually matching the nightly date.

**Real example this year:** in nightly-2026-03-28, the `#[must_use]` lint on `Result` tightened; hundreds of Rust libraries had to add `let _ =` prefixes. If our build broke on that nightly, a developer who pulled in the previous week would see green while a fresh clone would see red.

**Severity.** HIGH — build reproducibility is a blocker for:
1. Bisecting a conformance regression (which nightly introduced the divergence?)
2. Onboarding a new contributor (their nightly breaks, ours builds)
3. Deterministic binary distribution (the planned PyO3 wheel-build would produce different bytes depending on which nightly the builder used)

**Fix shape:**
1. `rust-toolchain.toml` pins the exact nightly date:
     [toolchain]
     channel = "nightly-2026-04-22"
     components = ["rustfmt", "clippy", "rust-src"]
2. Bump ritual: same cadence as the existing lib-updater sweep (see UPGRADE_LOG.md). Pin → sweep → test → commit. Monthly or on-demand.
3. CI uses `dtolnay/rust-toolchain@{{ rust-toolchain.toml channel }}` via the `toolchain` input so CI honors the pin rather than floating.
4. Document the pinning policy in AGENTS.md near the build-environment section.

Blast radius: rust-toolchain.toml (1 line change), ci.yml (1 line change), AGENTS.md (+1 paragraph). Zero source code changes.

This is a sibling issue to br-frankenpandas-8irq (pandas version pin) + br-frankenpandas-boyr (fixture pandas-version provenance). Same class: pin the reference, document the update ritual.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T19:28:53.722920769Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-22T23:13:37.776387738Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T23:13:37.776387738Z","close_reason":"Pinned the Rust nightly date in rust-toolchain.toml, taught CI to resolve that pin from the toolchain file, and documented the policy in AGENTS.md","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["infra","reproducibility","review","toolchain"]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-1e2i","title":"fp-io read_sql chunksize iterator","description":"Child slice of frankenpandas-fd90: add a chunked SQL read helper so callers can request pandas-style chunksize batches from read_sql_with_options output. Keep the existing SqlConnection trait, validate chunksize > 0, preserve column order/index shape per chunk, and add focused fp-io unit tests.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod-pandas","created_at":"2026-04-25T07:23:51.643578044Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T07:38:19.734796398Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T07:38:19.732947585Z","close_reason":"Completed in 28c8d61","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["io","parity","sql"]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-1gb1j","title":"fd90.167: add Pivot Tables integration test (locks in fd90.114 margins:bool fixes)","description":"fd90.74-166 follow-on. The README's 'Pivot Tables: Full Options' section (lines 1230-1249) hasn't been integration-tested. fd90.114 fixed the missing margins:bool args on pivot_table_with_margins/pivot_table_with_margins_name. Lock these in via integration test.

Fix: add 'readme_pivot_tables_compiles_and_runs' which mirrors the README example. Build a small DataFrame with revenue/region/product/quantity columns and verify:
  - df.pivot_table(values, index_col, columns_col, aggfunc)? — basic pivot
  - df.pivot_table_multi_values(&[values...], index, columns, aggfunc)?
  - df.pivot_table_with_margins(..., margins=true)? — with subtotals
  - df.pivot_table_with_margins_name(..., margins=true, name)? — custom label
  - df.pivot_table_fill(..., 0.0)? — NaN fill
  - df.pivot_table_multi_agg(values, index, columns, &[fns...])? — multi-fn

Pure additive test.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"test","created_at":"2026-04-26T16:29:23.458520991Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T16:31:28.493616002Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T16:31:28.490820237Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -14733,7 +14733,13 @@ pub struct DataFrame {
     #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
     row_multiindex: Option<fp_index::MultiIndex>,
     columns: BTreeMap<String, Column>,
-    #[serde(skip)]
+    // fd90.196: column_order is the user-visible insertion order. The
+    // previous `#[serde(skip)]` silently dropped it on round-trip,
+    // contradicting the README's "Round-trips perfectly" claim. Now
+    // serialized normally with `default` for backwards-compat with any
+    // pre-fd90.196 blobs (they'll deserialize with an empty Vec, which
+    // matches the prior behavior).
+    #[serde(default)]
     column_order: Vec<String>,
     #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
     column_multiindex: Option<fp_index::MultiIndex>,

@@ -48,6 +48,11 @@ sql-mysql = ["fp-io/sql-mysql"]
 tracing = ["fp-frame/tracing"]
 asupersync = ["fp-runtime/asupersync"]
 
+[dev-dependencies]
+# fd90.196: serde_json round-trip integration test for the README's
+# "Serialization and Interoperability" claim.
+serde_json = { workspace = true }
+
 [package.metadata.docs.rs]
 all-features = true
 rustdoc-args = ["--cfg", "docsrs"]
